disclaimer.short: AI insights are educational and not medical advice. If symptoms are severe, consider talking to a healthcare professional.
disclaimer.ai: Dialuna is a wellness companion, not a medical device. Answers are educational estimates based on your logged data.
disclaimer.full: Dialuna is a wellness and lifestyle app. It does not diagnose, treat, or prevent any medical condition, and it is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Cycle predictions and AI insights are estimates based on the information you log. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional about medical concerns, and never delay seeking care because of something you read in this app.
disclaimer.predictions: Predictions are estimates, not medical advice.

welcome.title: Dialuna
welcome.subtitle: A quiet place to know your body, cycle by cycle.

disclaimerScreen.title: A gentle note before we begin
disclaimerScreen.body:
Dialuna is a wellness companion. It helps you notice patterns in your cycle, mood, and energy.

It is not a medical device. It does not diagnose conditions, confirm pregnancy, or replace your doctor. Predictions are estimates that improve as you log.

cycleBasics.title: Let's set your baseline
cycleBasics.subtitle: You can always adjust these later in Settings.

symptoms.title: Symptoms you often notice
symptoms.subtitle: This helps Insights find patterns sooner.

account.subtitle: Your logs stay private, synced to this account only.

log.title: Daily log
log.subtitle: A minute of logging makes your forecasts smarter.
log.notePlaceholder: Anything else about today? Cravings, skin, thoughts…

reflection.menstrual: Thanks for logging, {{name}}. Cramps or low energy may fit your menstrual phase. Warmth, hydration, and rest can help today.
reflection.follicular: Thanks for logging, {{name}}. Your follicular phase often brings rising energy — a lovely time to build momentum.
reflection.ovulation: Thanks for logging, {{name}}. Around ovulation many people feel more social and confident. Enjoy the lift.
reflection.luteal: Thanks for logging, {{name}}. In the luteal phase mood and energy can soften. Gentle pacing counts as progress.
reflection.pms: Thanks for logging, {{name}}. Your entries may reflect your pre-period window. Extra rest, magnesium-rich food, and gentle movement may help.
reflection.symptomNote: You logged {{symptom}} — this may be related to your cycle. Not medical advice.

ai.title: AI Coach
ai.limitReached: You've used your 3 free questions today. Premium unlocks unlimited coaching.
ai.emptyTitle: Ask me anything about your cycle
ai.emptyBody: I use your logs and cycle phase to answer. Try a suggestion below.

prompts.p1: Why am I so tired today?
prompts.p4: What does my Hormone Twin score mean?

answer.tired: It makes sense you feel tired, {{name}}. You're on cycle day {{cycleDay}}, in your {{phase}}, and energy often dips here.
answer.workout: On cycle day {{cycleDay}} ({{phase}}), your body has its own pace for training.
answer.cravings: Cravings are very common in the {{phase}}, especially with your period about {{daysUntilPeriod}} days away.
answer.mood: Your mood can genuinely track your cycle. Right now you're in your {{phase}}, which often shapes emotional sensitivity.
answer.food: In your {{phase}}, food that steadies blood sugar and supports iron and magnesium tends to feel best.
answer.cramps: Cramps around your {{phase}} are common and usually cycle-related.
answer.week: Here's your week ahead, {{name}}: your period is estimated in about {{daysUntilPeriod}} days.
answer.sleep: Sleep can feel different across your cycle. In your {{phase}}, body temperature and hormones can affect how deeply you rest.
answer.general: Here's what your cycle suggests today, {{name}}: you're on day {{cycleDay}} in your {{phase}}.

why.menstrual: During your period, energy and iron levels are often at their lowest, so your body asks for more rest.
why.follicular: After your period, rising estrogen typically lifts energy, focus, and motivation.
why.ovulation: Around ovulation, many people feel a natural peak in confidence and social energy.
why.luteal: After ovulation, progesterone rises — this can soften energy and heighten emotional sensitivity.
why.pms: In the few days before your period, hormone shifts can raise stress sensitivity, cravings, and discomfort.

tired.high: Today, try steady hydration, a protein-rich breakfast, and a short walk in daylight to lift alertness.
tired.low: Today, honor the dip: lighter tasks, an earlier night, and gentle movement like stretching or a slow walk.

workout.high: Green light for stronger sessions — strength, cardio, or HIIT should feel good. Warm up well and listen to your body.
workout.low: Favor gentler movement today — yoga, walking, or light strength. Save max-effort HIIT for your higher-energy days.

cravings.high: Pair sweets with protein or fiber, keep water nearby, and don't skip meals — stable blood sugar calms cravings.
cravings.low: Try pairing something sweet with protein, magnesium-rich snacks like nuts or dark chocolate, and regular meals.

mood.high: Ride the good wave: plan social time or a task you've been putting off while your mood is supported.
mood.low: Be extra kind to yourself: reduce stimulation, journal for five minutes, and protect your sleep tonight.

food.high: Fresh, colorful meals with lean protein and complex carbs support your natural energy right now.
food.low: Warm, comforting meals with iron and magnesium — leafy greens, legumes, dark chocolate — may feel especially good.

cramps.high: Gentle heat, hydration, and light stretching can ease occasional cramps.
cramps.low: A warm compress, slow stretching, magnesium-rich food, and rest often help. Track it in your log so I can learn.

week.high: Expect stronger energy days ahead — a good window for demanding work and workouts before your next shift.
week.low: Energy may soften toward your period — front-load important tasks, plan gentler evenings, and prioritize sleep.

sleep.high: Keep your rhythm steady: consistent bedtime, morning light, and caffeine before noon.
sleep.low: Wind down earlier tonight: dim lights, a warm shower, and putting screens away 30 minutes before bed can deepen rest.

general.high: It's a supported time of your cycle — lean into energy while keeping meals and sleep steady.
general.low: It's a softer time of your cycle — pace yourself, hydrate, and let rest be productive.

coach.menstrual: Your body is doing quiet, important work. Slower movement, warmth, and earlier sleep can feel especially good today.
coach.follicular: Energy is on the rise. It's a natural window for fresh starts, harder workouts, and focused work.
coach.ovulation: You may feel a natural peak in confidence and social energy today. Enjoy it — and keep hydration up.
coach.luteal: Your body may prefer slower movement today. Try a walk, light stretching, and earlier sleep.
coach.pms: Your pre-period window may raise sensitivity and cravings. Gentle movement, magnesium-rich food, and rest can help.

menstrual: Iron-rich foods like spinach and lentils
Dark chocolate in small comforting doses
ovulation: Light meals with plenty of vegetables
Hydrating foods like cucumber and berries

follicular: Try something new — energy supports it
luteal: Lower intensity as your period nears

follicular: Plan something you look forward to
luteal: Lower the bar — done is enough

empty.title: Your patterns are loading…
empty.body: Log at least 3 days and Dialuna will start finding your personal patterns.

sleepCard.title: Sleep pattern
sleepCard.text: Your sleep quality averages {{pmsSleep}}/10 in your pre-period window vs {{otherSleep}}/10 otherwise.

pmsCard.title: Next PMS window
pmsCard.text: Estimated {{start}} – {{end}}. Plan gentler days if you can.

summaryCard.title: Monthly AI summary
summaryCard.text: This month, your energy tends to drop 3–4 days before your period. Sleep quality often dips in the same window. Gentle planning around those days may help.

locked.title: Deeper patterns are Premium
locked.body: Unlock energy, sleep, and PMS pattern analysis.

s1.title: Know your patterns
s1.body: Premium reveals how your energy, mood and focus move together across the full cycle — not just today.

s2.title: Your Hormone Twin, deeper
s2.body: See your score trend against women with cycles like yours, not just a single number.

s3.title: Never guess your PMS window
s3.body: Premium forecasts symptom-likely days two cycles out, so you can plan around them.
